specific_mechanism: MPSA: Marchenko-Pastur Spectral Allocation. Per attention sub-matrix (W_q, W_k, W_v, W_o), estimate the MP envelope [λ_-, λ_+] given empirical aspect ratio c. Count outlier singular values above λ_+ as `s_l`. Allocate LoRA rank r_l = round(R_total · s_l / Σ s_k) across layers.
llm_application: MPSA (Marchenko-Pastur Spectral Allocation) LoRA capacity-allocation rule: at the start of fine-tuning, for each layer l and each of W_q,W_k,W_v,W_o compute empirical eigenvalue distribution; fit MP envelope λ_+(σ²,c); count outliers `s_l = |{σ_i > λ_+(σ²,c)}|`; allocate r_l = round(R_total · s_l / Σ s_k). Pre-trained-layers with thin spectral tail get small LoRA rank (e.g., r=2); layers with rich outliers get r=32.
novelty_claim: MP-based allocation of LoRA rank is not standard; existing MP work in LLM context covers pruning (arXiv 2503.01922) and weight-dynamics analysis (arXiv 2507.12709), not as fine-tuning capacity allocator.
motivation_justification: MP distribution IS the rigorous eigenvalue law for random matrices; deviation from MP IS a measure of structural information. Bouchaud-Potters cleaning is the canonical RMT denoising tool applied to weight matrices.
content_words: random matrix theory bouchaud potters cleaning
weight spectrum analysis singular value count
MP envelope deviation structural information signal

llm_side: weight spectrum analysis singular value count
